module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one' (M2 main exercise, 5 phases)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+ on TEACHING beats; setup beats exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: P2-P5 mood_score=8; P1 mood_score=null per setup/scoping carve-out (mechanical pick-the-task). Anti-pattern callout 'Just a simple task that spans a few files. Not an epic.' at line 21 caps scope without paternalism.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 4 inlines Pocock's grill-me as plain prompt named 'a second agent that reads differently' (line 49). Maintainer block line 102: 'In M2 we inline the behaviour as a prompt; the skill reveal lands in M3 as authoring material, so we don't front-run M3's first-Skill-use moment.' Phase 5 line 75 names 'plan-mode approval inflation' retrospectively, not as preview. CLAUDE.local.md auto-load surface (line 77) is M1/M2 territory; M3 owns lazy-load (Skills). Clean sequencing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in PROMPTS, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Four fenced prompts (push-back-on-the-plan-1 plan request; -2 walk-down-branches; -3 name pattern + compare reads; -4 auto-load check) carry load-bearing actions. Body narrates context but speed-runners reading prompt-to-prompt still hit the moves.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text — no authored conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Zero hits for share-with-neighbor / compare-with-person / tell-the-room / take-90-seconds shapes. Body is about the artifact (plan file, push-back messages, sharpened plan). Trainer cohort-management in maintainer block Push-back moves (line 127+).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two-reads structure delivers n=2 by design: human read (P3 push-backs) and agent read (P4 walk-down) on SAME plan. Phase 5 line 71 explicitly names the gap: 'You catch what a human catches... The second read catches what an agent walking a decision tree catches.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure + recovering move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block Watch-fors (lines 107-114) covers P3 rubber-stamp / performative / softening / timer-abuse / senior-refusal each with diagnostic + push-back move; P4 drift; P5 deflection. Decision points (lines 116-120) cover P3 under-3-min and over-12-min. Push-back moves (lines 127-134) map per phase. Coverage thorough.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic — plans get rubber-stamped without it.
rules_evaluated.evidence: P3 forces two push-backs via 'keep planning with feedback' before approval branch available; P4 forces 5-12 question walk-down with persona rejecting some. Line 33: 'Don't approve yet.' Plan-mode-approval-inflation IS the failure this rule names; exercise engineered to defeat it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task selection needs floor AND ceiling explicitly named in body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 19-21 name floor and ceiling positively ('feature slice, small migration, targeted refactor — something where touching the wrong file matters'), then bolded anti-pattern: 'Just a simple task that spans a few files. Not an epic.' Exercise-time placement per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session widget canonical shape; place by learning flow.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5: '**Session** *(new, "Module 2 - Plan mode done right")*' + line 9-11 /rename slug. Exercise-top pattern (exercise opens module's first session). Renderer wraps inner pre in .rt-cli for CLI-only rename.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 13 '**What you do:**' names activity (run through plan mode, push back, hand to second agent, approve, stop) + artifact (approved plan) + constraint (don't execute). 'That gap is the skill this module is building' names module's overall capability, not a specific prompt's REVEAL.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block (lines 85-146) has Watch-fors, Decision points, Push-back moves, Arc, Frameworks but no dedicated 'Leap test:' line naming three observable Monday-morning verbs (e.g., 'opens plan mode on a real feature without prompting'; 'sends two specific push-backs before approving a real plan'; 'invokes a walk-down second read before merging a non-trivial plan'). Rule originated 2026-05-03 from gap-scan; sibling exercises should carry this.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test:' block in maintainer with three observable verbs the engineer exhibits on their own codebase by next Monday — concrete actions, not competencies. TODO, non-blocking.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise file, not a lecture. The exercise itself ends with a recognition beat (Phase 5 names pattern after living it) — closer-shape applied to exercise structure, consistent with rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 81 hands off to Debrief Compound step + personal CLAUDE.local.md. Maintainer Arc section (lines 137-139) names M3 pickup: 'the push-backs the student sent at P3 are the raw material for M3's first judge ("did the fix really land?"). The push-back discipline becomes the gate spec.' Strategy §M3 detail confirms M3 owns first judges/skills/subagents.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M2 grounded-competence doesn't steal M3 earned-trust.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 64: 'Don't execute the plan. The work of making it good is the exercise. Execution is next module's concern.' Execution-free design protects M3's territory — no premature trust-build, no Q+S preview. Mood lands but does not resolve.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors strategy's per-module mood and discipline framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy §M2: 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it, and I know the move is two reads, not one.' File line 16: 'The point: two reads, paired. Execution is next module's concern.' Direct hit; felt-evidence lands through contrast at Phase 5 line 71. Discipline lead, no failure-mode framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment — paired reads recognition lands.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 5 (line 66-75) has Claude name the design pattern; 'plan-mode approval inflation' is the label the exercise hands the student AFTER they've already defeated it. Recognition, not lecture. The forcing functions (two push-backs + walk-down) survive reasonable persona-skill variation — even the senior-refusing-the-forcing-function case has a maintainer-block accommodation (defense IS the read).
